    <h1 {...{
      "id": "inter-plugin-communication"
    }}>{`Inter Plugin Communication`}</h1>
    <p>{`UXP allows communication between plugins installed within the same application`}</p>
    <p>{`This feature is handy when you know another plugin already automates a specific task; in such cases, you can invoke it instead of duplicating the effort. To provide the best user experience, ensure your plugin clearly communicates such dependencies.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<strong parentName="p">{`Plugin Manager`}</strong>{` module provides the necessary APIs to establish the connection. Before diving into an example, make sure you're familiar with the following topics:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../concepts/entrypoints/index.md"
        }}>{`Plugin Entrypoints`}</a></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../concepts/manifest/index.md#permissionsdefinition"
        }}>{`Manifest Permissions`}</a></li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Additionally, you will need to have a basic understanding of the plugin you are going to communicate with, which includes:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The plugin's `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`id`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`entrypoints`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The structure of arguments to be passed, if applicable.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "implementation"
    }}>{`Implementation`}</h2>
    <p>{`We’ll refer to the two plugins as `}<strong parentName="p">{`Requester`}</strong>{` and `}<strong parentName="p">{`Responder`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "requester"
    }}>{`Requester`}</h3>
    <p>{`This is the plugin that will initiate the communication with the Responder. It `}<strong parentName="p">{`must have`}</strong>{` the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../concepts/manifest/index.md#ipcpermission"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`enablePluginCommunication`}</inlineCode></a>{` permission set to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`true`}</inlineCode>{` in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` file.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  // ...
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "ipc": { "enablePluginCommunication": true }
  }
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Through the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`pluginManager`}</inlineCode>{` module, the Requester plugin can get a list of all installed plugins in Premiere and find the Responder plugin by its `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id`}</inlineCode>{` (which must be known in advance).`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const { pluginManager } = require("uxp");

const allPlugins = pluginManager.plugins;
const responderPlugin = Array.from(allPlugins)
  .find(plugin => plugin.id === "Test-responder"); // 👈 Responder id
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`When you've stored a reference to the Responder plugin, you can invoke its `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`command`}</inlineCode>{` entrypoints using the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`invokeCommand()`}</inlineCode>{`, or request to show its panel using the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`showPanel()`}</inlineCode>{` methods. Pass the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id`}</inlineCode>{` of the entrypoint you want to invoke or show.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`responderPlugin.invokeCommand("simpleCommand");
responderPlugin.showPanel("simplePanel");
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text, text2" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Payloads`}</p>
    <p>{`It is possible to pass data using the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`invokeCommand()`}</inlineCode>{` method:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const payload = { message: "From Requester" };
responderPlugin.invokeCommand("simpleCommand", payload);
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "responder"
    }}>{`Responder`}</h3>
    <p>{`This is the plugin that will receive the communication from the Requester plugin. It does not need additional manifest permissions, but the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`command`}</inlineCode>{` entrypoints the Requester will invoke must be exposed in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` file.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  // ...
  "entrypoints": [
    {
      "id": "simplePanel",
      "type": "panel",
      "label": { "default": "Main Panel" },
      // ...
    },
    {
      "id": "simpleCommand",
      "type": "command",
      "label": { "default": "Simple Command" }
    },
    {
      "id": "commandWithInput",
      "type": "command",
      "label": { "default": "Command With Input" }
    }
  ],
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`The commands should also be implemented in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`index.js`}</inlineCode>{` file. For more information, please refer to the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../add-commands/index.md"
      }}>{`Add Commands`}</a>{` tutorial.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "example"
    }}>{`Example`}</h2>
    <p>{`In this example, the Requester plugin implements a button that will initiate three requests to the Responder plugin:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`show the Responder's panel`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`invoke the Responder's `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`simpleCommand`}</inlineCode>{` entrypoint`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`invoke the Responder's `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`commandWithInput`}</inlineCode>{` entrypoint, with an input payload`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`The Responder plugin will log the requests in the panel's body.`}</p>

    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexhtml"
    }}>{`index.html`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <script src="main.js"></script>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css" />
</head>
<body>
  <sp-heading>Requester Plugin</sp-heading>
  <sp-divider></sp-divider>
  <div class="main-div">
    <sp-body id="plugin-body">
      <sp-button id="btnCommunicate">

    <h4 {...{
      "id": "stylecss"
    }}>{`style.css`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-css"
      }}>{`body { padding: 16px; color: white; }

sp-divider { margin-bottom: 20px; }

sp-heading { color: var(--uxp-host-text-color-secondary, white); }

`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`//global objects.
const { pluginManager } = require("uxp");

document
  .querySelector("#btnCommunicate")
  .addEventListener("click", communicateWithAnotherPlugin);

function communicateWithAnotherPlugin() {
  try {
    const allPlugins = pluginManager.plugins;
    const plugin = Array.from(allPlugins).find(
      (plugin) => plugin.id === "Test-responder"
    );
    if (plugin && plugin.enabled) {
      console.log("All commands:", plugin.manifest.commands);
      console.log("All panels:", plugin.manifest.panels);

      // Show the plugin panel
      // Note that panels can only be made visible
      // You can't ask to hide a panel
      plugin.showPanel("simplePanel");

      // Invoke the command
      plugin.invokeCommand("simpleCommand");

      // Send an payload to the command
      const payload = { message: "Stay hydrated!" };
      // Invoke the command with the payload
      plugin.invokeCommand("commandWithInput", payload.message);
    } else {
      // Prompt the user to install/enable the plugin before trying again
    }
  } catch (e) {
    console.error(e);
  }
}

`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{

    "clipboard": "readAndWrite",

  "entrypoints": [

      "id": "starterpanel",
      "type": "panel",
      "minimumSize": { "width": 430, "height": 500 },
      "maximumSize": { "width": 2000, "height": 2000 },
      "preferredDockedSize": { "width": 230, "height": 300 },
      "preferredFloatingSize": { "width": 400, "height": 300 },
      "label": { "default": "PremierePro IPC Panel" },
      "icons": [
        {
          "width": 23, "height": 23,
          "path": "icons/dark.png", "scale": [1,2],
          "theme": [ "darkest", "dark", "medium" ]
        },
        {
          "width": 23, "height": 23,
          "path": "icons/light.png", "scale": [1,2],
          "theme": [ "lightest", "light" ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "icons": [
    {
      "width": 48, "height": 48, "path": "icons/plugin-icon.png",
      "scale": [1,2],
      "theme": [ "darkest", "dark", "medium", "lightest", "light", "all" ],
      "species": [ "pluginList" ]
    }
  ]
}
`}</code></pre>

    <h4 {...{

    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-css"
      }}>{`body { color: white; padding: 16px; }

sp-divider { margin-bottom: 20px; }

li:before { content: "• "; width: 3em; }

#plugin-body {
  color: var(--uxp-host-text-color-secondary, white);
  height: 220px; margin-top: 5px; border: 1px solid #808080;
  border-radius: 4px; padding: 16px; overflow: scroll;
}

sp-heading { color: var(--uxp-host-text-color-secondary, white); }

footer {
  display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; justify-content: flex-end; }

footer > * { margin: 5px; }

footer div { margin-bottom: 1em; width: 100%; }

.main-div { position: relative; }

.clear-btn {
  display: none; position: absolute; top: 10px; right: 6px; cursor: pointer;
}

.main-div:hover .clear-btn { display: inline; }
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-1"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`//global objects.
const { entrypoints } = require("uxp");

entrypoints.setup({
  commands: {
    simpleCommand: () => doThing(),
    commandWithInput: (args) => doThing(args),
  },
  panels: { simplePanel: { show(rootNode) {} },
  },
});

// Log messages to the panel's body
function logToPanel(msg) {
  const bodyElement = document.getElementById("plugin-body");
  const message =
    msg === undefined
      ? \`Generic message from Requester Plugin <br />\`
      : \`<span>Custom message from Requester Plugin: \${msg}</span><br />\`;
  bodyElement.innerHTML += message;
}

// Commands handler
function doThing(args) {
  console.log("payload", args);
  // Pass a payload (if any) to the panel
  logToPanel(args && args.data[0]);
}

// Clear the panel's body
document.getElementById("clear-btn").addEventListener("click", () => {
  const bodyElement = document.getElementById("plugin-body");
  if (bodyElement) {
    bodyElement.innerHTML = "";
  }
});

`}</code></pre>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "additional-notes"
    }}>{`Additional notes`}</h2>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`You `}<strong parentName="li">{`may not see any error if the entrypoint is not found`}</strong>{`. We recommend using `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`plugin.manifest.commands`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`plugin.manifest.panels`}</inlineCode>{` to select from the actual list of entrypoints.
Users may have turned off a particular plugin via the Adobe Creative Cloud Desktop App. Before invoking it, check the plugin's availability by using `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`plugin.enabled`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`You cannot pass methods in the payload object.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Cross-application communication is not supported (e.g., Premiere to Photoshop).`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "summary"
    }}>{`Summary`}</h2>
    <p>{`Inter-plugin communication enables UXP plugins to invoke commands and show panels from other installed plugins within Adobe Premiere.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Key Concepts:`}</strong></p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Requester plugin`}</strong>{`: This plugin initiates communication and requires the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`enablePluginCommunication: true`}</inlineCode>{` permission in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{`.`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Plugin Manager`}</strong>{`: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`pluginManager.plugins`}</inlineCode>{` to discover and reference other installed plugins by their `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`id`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Communication methods`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`invokeCommand()`}</inlineCode>{` to execute commands and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`showPanel()`}</inlineCode>{` to display panels from the target plugin.`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Responder plugin`}</strong>{`: This plugin receives communication requests, exposes entrypoints in its manifest (no special permissions are needed), and implements the entrypoints in its code.`}</li>
    </ol>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
;
MDXContent.isMDXComponent = true;
